minimal_context: The student is instructed to line up and be quiet while transitioning between classrooms.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please line up and be quiet for the transition.

STUDENT: (Continues talking instead of being quiet)

TUTOR: I still hear people talking!

STUDENT: (Refuses to line up) We're going on strike!

TUTOR: You need to get in line right now.

STUDENT: (Sings loudly) Oakdale school, is not the best school for you. I hate Oakdale, I hate Oakdale.

options.A: Ignores the student's behavior and takes the rest of the line of students to the next classroom.
options.B: Uses a "request for clarification," asking the student for explanations and elaborations about the transition.
options.C: Expresses empathy toward the student using verbalizations like, "What's the matter?" and "Did you not want to line up?"
options.D: Shows the student two picture cards with movement actions and asks them to choose how to move to the next classroom.
